hypothesis: Superhydrides of s–d border metals with non-integer hydrogen-to-metal ratios that produce coexisting hydrogen motifs (polyhedral cages plus two-dimensional hydrogen sheets), exemplified by M4H52, will exhibit electron–phonon coupling constants λ at least 20% larger and superconducting transition temperatures Tc at least 20 K higher than the most stable integer-stoichiometry clathrate phases (e.g., MH10 or MH9) of the same metal at comparable pressures.
